local_names.romanized_local: Sū Jí Dàngāo (Chinese); Kek Sugee (Malay); Sugee Kek (Tamil romanized)

region_of_origin: Singapore — Eurasian community of Portuguese-Straits heritage
food_subgroup: Dense semolina and almond butter cake — made with fine semolina (suji), ground almonds, butter, and egg yolks

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no concept for Sugee Cake. No exact URI exists. Closest parent concepts are butter cake (FOODON:03301826) or semolina-based baked product. Null is used honestly pending a community submission.
ontology.foodex2_description: Cakes, pastries and similar products — FoodEx2 Level 1 anchor for baked sweet goods. Sugee Cake is a baked semolina-almond butter cake; no more specific FoodEx2 code exists. A000E is the defensible closest-parent anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code exists specifically for Sugee Cake or semolina-almond cake. A000E (cakes and pastries) is the closest defensible parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for Sugee Cake or Eurasian semolina cake. Null used honestly.

preparation_method: folding in semolina and ground almonds

serving.description: 1 slice (approximately 1/12 of a standard 20 cm loaf)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard slice of Sugee Cake weighs approximately 80 g, equivalent to roughly one-twelfth of a standard 20 cm loaf pan cake.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — SR Legacy component derivation for Sugee Cake (semolina, ground almonds, butter, egg yolks, sugar); no direct database entry exists for Sugee Cake
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service (ARS) — FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct USDA or HPB Singapore entry exists for Sugee Cake. Values are derived from USDA SR Legacy component data for fine semolina (USDA FDC 169737), ground almonds (USDA FDC 170567), unsalted butter (USDA FDC 173410), egg yolks (USDA FDC 172185), and caster sugar (USDA FDC 169655), weighted by a representative recipe ratio (approximately 35% semolina, 25% ground almonds, 25% butter, 10% egg yolks, 5% sugar by dry weight before baking). Confidence is medium due to recipe variability across Eurasian family traditions.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281–2283 — analogous estimate using semolina-based foods (GI ~55) and almond-modified baked goods
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Sugee Cake in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 58 is estimated from: (1) semolina-based foods GI ~55 (University of Sydney GI Database — coarse semolina porridge, GI 55); (2) almond-enriched baked goods which reduce GI by approximately 5–10 units relative to white-flour equivalents due to fat, protein, and fibre from almonds; (3) standard butter cake GI ~74 as upper reference. The estimate of GI 58 is conservative and defensible. GL per serving = (58 × 40.5 × 0.80) / 100 = 18.8 g for an 80 g slice.

hypertension.key_factor: High saturated fat (12.5 g/100g) and moderate caloric density (445 kcal/100g); sodium is low at 90 mg/100g
hypertension.guidance: Sodium content is low (90 mg per 100 g) and does not pose a direct hypertensive risk, but the high saturated fat content (12.5 g/100g from butter) warrants portion control in patients with hypertension-associated cardiovascular risk; limit to one 80 g slice on festive occasions.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium GI (58) and moderate glycemic load per serving (GL 18.8 for 80 g slice); sugar content 22 g/100g
type2_diabetes.guidance: Sugee Cake has a medium GI of approximately 58 — meaningfully lower than standard white-flour butter cake (GI ~74) — due to semolina's denser particle structure and the fibre and protein contributed by ground almonds; however, the GL of 18.8 per 80 g slice is moderate and individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit to half a slice (40 g) and pair with a protein source to further blunt the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (12.5 g/100g), cholesterol (115 mg/100g), and trans fat (0.3 g/100g) from butter; partially offset by monounsaturated fat and vitamin E from almonds
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The saturated fat content of 12.5 g per 100 g and cholesterol of 115 mg per 100 g are clinically significant for patients with cardiovascular disease; an 80 g slice delivers approximately 10 g saturated fat, approaching the daily limit for high-risk patients, and should be consumed only occasionally and in small portions; the ground almond content provides vitamin E (4.5 mg/100g) and monounsaturated fat which partially offset the atherogenic risk from butter.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Sugee Cake (苏极蛋糕; Kek Sugee) is the signature festive cake of Singapore's Eurasian-Peranakan community — a dense, moist, crumbly celebration cake made from fine semolina (suji or sugee), ground almonds, generous quantities of unsalted butter, and a large number of egg yolks, typically perfumed with brandy and vanilla extract. The word 'sugee' (also spelled 'suji' or 'sugi') derives from the Gujarati and Hindi word for semolina, reflecting the multicultural heritage of Singapore's Eurasian community, who are descended from Portuguese-Straits settlers who intermarried with local Peranakan and other Asian communities from the 16th century onward. Unlike a standard sponge cake or butter cake, Sugee Cake has a distinctively sandy, slightly grainy, and very dense crumb texture imparted by the semolina, which does not develop gluten in the same way as wheat flour. The cake is typically baked in a loaf pan and served in thick slices at Christmas, weddings, and baptisms. It is rarely available commercially and is primarily home-baked from closely guarded family heirloom recipes, though a small number of specialty Eurasian restaurants and bakeries in Singapore — including Quentin's Eurasian Restaurant and The White Rabbit — offer it seasonally. Nutritionally, Sugee Cake is energy-dense at 445 kcal per 100 g, with significant fat (28.5 g, of which 12.5 g is saturated from butter), moderate protein (9.5 g from eggs and almonds), and moderate carbohydrate (40.5 g). The use of semolina rather than white flour lowers the glycemic index to approximately 58 (medium GI), and the ground almonds contribute dietary fibre (3.5 g/100g), vitamin E (4.5 mg/100g), magnesium (45 mg/100g), and monounsaturated fat. Clinically, it is a moderate-GI, high-saturated-fat festive food best consumed in small portions by individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease.

confidence_reason: No direct entry for Sugee Cake exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, ICMR-NIN, or IMR Malaysia MyFCD. Nutrient values are derived from USDA SR Legacy component data for the key ingredients (semolina, ground almonds, butter, egg yolks, sugar) weighted by a representative recipe ratio. GI is an analogous estimate from semolina-based foods in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium because recipe variability across Eurasian family traditions is high and no direct laboratory analysis of Sugee Cake has been published in an approved source.
notes: Vitamin C is 0.0 mg/100g — confirmed zero, as Sugee Cake contains no vitamin-C-bearing ingredients (no fruit, no vegetables); this is naturally zero consistent with the ingredient profile. Trans fat of 0.3 g/100g is attributed to naturally occurring trans fats in butter (vaccenic acid and conjugated linoleic acid) rather than industrial hydrogenation. Halal status is marked false because traditional recipes include brandy; alcohol-free versions using rose water exist and would be halal-suitable. The two portions in the input (60 g and 80 g) have been resolved to 80 g as the default serving, consistent with the verified source annotation. Monounsaturated fat (10.2 g) and polyunsaturated fat (3.8 g) are estimated from USDA SR Legacy almond and butter component data and are not in the original input; they are flagged as estimated. FoodEx2 code A000E is used as the closest defensible parent anchor for cakes and pastries; no more specific code exists for semolina-almond cake. Water_g is estimated at 18.5 g/100g based on USDA SR Legacy moisture values for analogous baked butter-semolina goods (butter cake moisture approximately 20–22 g/100g, reduced slightly for the denser semolina-almond formulation); the USDA component derivation does not provide a direct composite water value, so this is an estimated figure consistent with the macronutrient balance (energy_kcal 445 is consistent with approximately 18–20 g water in a high-fat baked product).
